title: Stratus XA2000 'Once Again, Stratus Catches the Competition With Their Computers Down' — ABA Banking Journal Ad, 1987 (PSK as named contact)
author: Stratus Computer (corporate marketing — Kastner-era)
abstract: Full-page Stratus XA2000-family launch advertisement reproduced from the ABA Banking Journal. Headline: 'ONCE AGAIN, STRATUS CATCHES THE COMPETITION WITH THEIR COMPUTERS DOWN.' Photograph shows five competitor-executives in despair. Ad introduces the **Stratus XA2000 family** (Models 110, 120, 130, 140) as the most powerful fault-tolerant computers in the world, with Model 140 executing 50+ transactions/second — three times the processing power of the previous Stratus XA600. All XA2000 systems backward-compatible with older Stratus systems. Body copy emphasizes open-ended architecture for future scaling, customer-loyalty stat ("100% of customers surveyed would not even consider changing computer companies"), modular online upgrades, and 21st-century time horizon. Footer: **'For complete information, contact your local Stratus sales office, or call Peter Kastner at (617) 460-2192.'** Address: Stratus Computer, 55 Fairbanks Boulevard, Marlboro, MA 01752.
importance_rationale: Primary-source XA2000 launch marketing collateral with Kastner as the named contact, reproduced in ABA Banking Journal — the leading FT-banking-vertical trade publication. Companion to study #6 (1985 manufacturing ad) showing Kastner as the cross-vertical Stratus customer-relations contact across multiple ad campaigns.
relevance_rationale: Specific product is obsolete; broader pattern of customer-loyalty/lock-in claims and modular online upgrades remain durable marketing themes.
prescience_rationale: Stratus' 21st-century positioning ('you may not see another computer like this until the 21st century') was proven correct in the narrow sense that Stratus systems remained in service into the 21st century. The customer-lock-in claim (100% would not switch) proved durably accurate for the Stratus VOS installed base.
